item.title: NO, YOU MOVE.
item.description: “Doesn't matter what the press says. Doesn't matter what the politicians or the mobs say. Doesn't matter if the whole country decides that something wrong is something right. This nation was founded on one principle above all else: The requirement that we stand up for what we believe, no matter the odds or the consequences. When the mob and the press and the whole world tell you to move, your job is to plant yourself like a tree beside the river of truth, and tell the whole world -- "No, YOU move.” SEMPER FI DEVIL DOG
item.dc:creator: TheRealSauceBoss

item.title: 110 Service Members Filed Complaints That Commanders Said Trump Was Anointed By Jesus To Spark Armageddon. The First Amendment Would Like A Fucking Word.
item.description:
110 U.S. service members. 40 units. 30 military bases. All filing complaints that commanders told them the Iran war is "part of God's divine plan" and Trump was "anointed by Jesus to spark Armageddon." This is not medieval history. This is Tuesday. ?
The Founders built a wall between church and state because they'd seen what happens when generals take orders from God instead of the Constitution. It's called a Crusade. Crusades have a body count. The First Amendment was not a fucking suggestion. It was a warning. ⚔️?? #FirstAmendment #ChurchAndState #MilitaryReligion #MRFF #ConstitutionalCrisis #ItWasAWarningNotASuggestion

item.title: Potato's Daily Dirties
item.description:
The above image was made by the wonderful CammyJay! They have their own channel in the deviousness discord where they share artwork and sometimes do drawings based on word prompts given by members. its a lot of fun! I has a Deviousness discord server! We do have a hard rule of no politics and/or religion. The discord is a safe space to get away from the bullshit going on. We have a bunch of areas including a SFW area, content creator area, a NSFW area and more! We have gaming fun! We have spontaneous gaming and some ping roles for people to use to get groups together based on US and EU time zones! We have movie nights on Saturdays! So feel free to wander in and say haii! Thank you to everyone who has joined and made it such a unique and special place! Deviousness discord that is 18+: https://discord.gg/deviousness If you have issues with link see dis: https://imgur.com/a/8GUNAz4 Deviousness steam group: https://steamcommunity.com/groups/DeviousnessDiscord
artist of work above (aside from my signing potato on it): https://florahv.artstation.com/projects/XnL8P3 I am on dis now and trying to remember to linky over there too for when I post and also post the memes that get removed: https://bsky.app/profile/potatocheesmonk.bsky.social I has a post with links to all my past posts. enjoy: https://imgur.com/a/ZDA2CBe #potato #memes #dailydirties #makinmywaydowntown #holdinhandstothefrontpage
item.dc:creator: PotatoCheesMonk

1
2

17 science is green, math is red
18
19
20
21
22
23
24
25
26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @ftentor did this one! Check out their other posts for more cool artwork.
27 Man Tax: By request, Elliot Page
“Contrary to the rumors that you've heard, I was not born in a manger. I was actually born on Krypton and sent here by my father, Jor-El, to save the planet Earth.” - Barack Obama So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 ***Also if you miss abbiistabbii's discord community good news! Here's the new one https://discord.gg/NPStJGgARq - where everyone's reconnecting, shitposting, and hanging out again*** https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. (Apparently doesn't work in firefox). ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me

item.title: Every Word Except The One That Would Have Ended This Immediately. Funny How That Fucking Works.
item.description: Let me get this straight. The Secretary of Homeland Security was asked under OATH if she was sleeping with Corey Lewandowski. She called it "outrageous." She expressed shock at "tabloid garbage." She did literally everything EXCEPT say the word NO. IN A CONGRESSIONAL HEARING. UNDER OATH. ?? She then said "I am shocked we are peddling tabloid garbage." MADAM YOU ARE THE SECRETARY OF HOMELAND FUCKING SECURITY. You have a secret bag. You have a Lewandowski. You have a husband who apparently receives information on a need-to-know basis. THIS IS A NATIONAL SECURITY ISSUE!! ? And the husband is just... THERE. Sitting at home. Finding out his wife's extracurricular activities via a Congressional hearing like the rest of us. Sir. SIR. Are you okay?? Do you need someone to call?? We can recommend a lawyer. Several, actually. ?⚖️? The woman responsible for protecting America's borders cannot secure her own marriage, is carrying secret bags on flights with her alleged boyfriend, and responded to questions about it under oath with "I didn't hear a no." THESE ARE THE ADULTS RUNNING THE COUNTRY. God help us all. ???? #KristiNoem #HomelandSecurity #FindingOutLive #NeedToKnowBasis #TheHusbandHasQuestions #SirAreYouOkay #CallALawyer #NotablyNotNo
item.dc:creator: JamieGeorge

item.title: She Brought The Receipts. The White House Brought Vibes And A Strongly Worded Relationship With Reality.
item.description:
BREAKING: Journalism briefly remembered its job. ? Kaitlan Collins showed up with receipts while Karoline Leavitt brought the usual White House starter pack: vibes, deflection, and a confident relationship with fiction. Turns out facts still exist — they just have to fight their way through the spin cycle. ?
? #BlueCrew #KaitlanCollins #KarolineLeavitt #ConfidentRelationshipWithFiction
item.dc:creator: JamieGeorge

item.title: Body painted myself as Pennywise - Inspired by Jamie Tyndall
item.description:
I AM IN THE UK AND HAVE BEEN BLOCKED FROM USING IMGUR!!! PLEASE FIND ME ON OTHER SOCIALS I AM TRYINGGGGGGG I'm on twitch most of the time btw PLEASE find me on https://linktr.ee/mcroft07?utm_source=linktree_profile_share since I only post a small selection of my work here. I HATE THE GOVERNMENTS TOO Faq: I use body paint and do these in a mirror No, it is not AI I just looked at pictures and did what I wanted with the design tbh. I use professional body paint covers (pasties) this means I am not wearing a top but I am also not naked. I paint myself, I make the props myself, I film myself, and I also edit. This is my full time thing now (yayy) I paint these by looking in a mirror, with brushes, and paint It takes between 6-12 hours each Body painting myself is my hobby/passion I do own clothes It doesn’t tickle “Why don’t you just wear clothes” once again, body painting myself is my hobby/passion. I am painting my skin, I am not wearing a suit - but that does not mean I am naked. I know, even I think this is a ridiculous set of questions I have to constantly answer I did not go to art school, I am self taught. Talent gets you so far, but this is a learned skill. Thank you to everyone who wants to help me with VPNs etc but please note that I am highly concerned about someone doxxing me so I will not accept any offer on this. I also don't want to pay for a VPN to use ONE website, so I just click through until I can get through, if I can get through, post and interact with the community for a while then turn it off. This is a real threat to me, I have been grabbed in the streets and had people attempt to follow me home before, so I take safety online and offline very seriously. The paint isn’t edible (gross) I've been painting myself for 16 years. The 07 does not mean I was born in 2007, UK 7 is my shoe size No, I don't need help painting my back, I don't want to paint my back, I don't need to paint my back. (also gross) No, I don't need help washing it off. (also you are gross) If you want to see more I am MCroft07 on IG, twitter, Tiktok, YouTube I paint them live on twitch I like doing the lip syncs, they're fun for me while I paint them. I couldn’t care less if you think they’re cringe. I am cringe but I am free My tattoos on my ribs are from Tomb Raider - I am a big TR fan I do not have an OF but if you want to send me money THAT badly you can just tip my twitch account. Haha. The shower afterwards takes about 15 minutes, I use a regular sponge and shower gel. The reason why it can look different at the end is because I am wearing a wig, I promise you it is not a different person because the hair changed. While on this topic, if my eyes change colour it’s because I am wearing contact lenses. (Those with an IQ above 10… I know. Even I’m shocked the wig thing is a frequent comment). I use body paint. It doesn’t tickle, I am very ticklish and this is fine. I don’t have an allergic reaction to the paint, but if you do it, you should always do a patch test first I do not know where you can buy body paint in every country. I know in the UK it is available at FacePaintingshop.com. I use a plethora of different brands, it’s best to search for them yourself online. I don’t work in Hollywood, I don’t want to. I just want to improve my body painting for myself. I am English, not American. I do not go outside like this to scare people. 1, that would be very cold. 2, in this current age, everyone would take photos without me knowing and it would likely dox me :)))))) I’m not a sweaty person, but if I was this would be a lot more difficult. Thank you to those of you who came from Imgur to my Twitch channel! “What’s the point in painting yourself when you can wear clothes?” all my clothes are in the laundry, had to resort to this, tough life It would not be quicker to jump into a pool of paint - think of the drying time?! Really proud of myself because I just bought a cheap top from Vinted and it was too small so I changed the back from a zipper to a corset style top to make it bigger to fit me and it worked! My secondary school textiles teacher would be proud of me! I don’t care that I wash it off at the end, I get to reuse this canvas, we love a reduce, reuse, recycle queen. I am not the lady who did the Maneskin video, it’s a cool video and she slayed, but it isn’t me. No it isn't AI, I fucking hate AI I used to do nothing with my hands and people told me I wasn't really painting, so I started painting bits while lip syncing and while recording and doing the transitions and people still hate it so honestly go fuck yourself and don't watch my shit then you're boring I can promise you there is not *a man* painting these and I am pretending to paint myself. I am genuinely painting myself. To everyone who thinks I paint it "wrong" you do it, show me it and I will critique it. The difference is that I will try and put myself out there, I respect artists to grow themselves and find their own way and style because I am an artist, not some blindsided bystander buffoon who thinks they know more about a subject they've never tried. Check yourself with your unsolicited advice and criticism. On that topic, I can handle criticism which is usually the defence people take when I point out that I did not ask. I know a lot of you don’t like reading this faq because it is horrid to see that I have to answer all of these. Unfortunately I have noticed that when I don’t put in all the icky things then I get more gross comments and I just have to spend more time blocking people so it’s better to call it out. I just copy and paste it each time anyway.
This is Jamie Tyndall's work, he has a lot of cool artwork and I think his style and costume design for characters is really cool and inspiring so I based mine on this one. You can find more of his work here: https://jamietyndall.net/products/ahsoka-print-2

item.title: The Story of Coastal Critters
item.description:
How amazing is this entry? Coastal Critters is an outdoor sanctuary for positive, long-term medical, seniors, and feral cats!!
This sits on 19 acres, and they spent millions and millions of dollars on fencing. Everything is kitty-proof, and the rescue spans over 10 acres.
They prioritize scared and unwanted cats who majority come from colonies. They have a great adoption program for friendlies, but typically put all their effort into feral or FIV/FELV-positive cats who are not friendly. This is their inside ward.
They even have a senior section on the property. This sweet blind cat was found in the middle of a highway and was unable to see. They saved him and brought him to the sanctuary. I definitely fell in love—so many times.
Every structure and every building has heating and air conditioning. Ensuring comfortable living conditions year-round. A lot of them came to them untouchable, but over time have flipped and started accepting nose boops and chin scratches. No matter how scared or how broken, they have a place, HERE!
They even partnered with the state of Alabama to ensure feral cats on the beach have a safe place to go when trapped. (How amazing that they chose life-saving programs instead of euthanasia!?) We have a breed of mouse that is endangered and stray cats are among the most predatory animals. Thankfully, they have a really great working relationship, and these cats get the best medical care and a place to live out their lives well, also preserving this sweet little beach mouse.
If I were a stray cat and didn't like humans, this would be the place I'd wanna go. Actually, as a human, this is already where I wanna go and live every single day. There are so many loving cats and so many who are still scared, but have a wonderful place to live their life. I was amazed by the Fencing and precautions put in place to ensure the cats stay within the sanctuary and to keep predators out.
They have expanded and started saving critters, as they call them. These sweet horses were saved from the slaughterhouse to be used in a parade, then returned to the slaughterhouse, until the sweet rescue stepped in and saved them. I love them and will forever think about them. Bravo, Coastal Critters, you were doing an amazing job. #goals
item.dc:creator: AlabamaAnimalRescuer

item.title: The Truth about SNAP and Trump's 2026 Disgusting "Reforms"
item.description:
The changes below are for FLORIDA.
Here's the list of nation wide reforms.
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/InmY4qSsNgQ https://www.fns.usda.gov/snap/waivers/foodrestriction Over 40million American's are on SNAP. Many of which have full time jobs but a little extra help to make ends meet. Almost all of Wal-Mart workers, one of the largest employers in America, are on SNAP. Along with many other large employers such as Amazon and Dollar General.
item.dc:creator: Imadethisaccounttopost

item.title: During The Troubles, a very troubling event occurred that proved just how dangerous an impotent police force can be
item.description:
I'm still working on Part 2 to The Radium Girls. It's taking me longer because I'm going through over 3,000 archived documents. However, this was another post on my queque. This movie scene has stuck with me since I first watched it years ago. It’s more unsettling to me that it’s a true story. This picture is a screenshot from “In The Name Of The Father,” a 1993 biographical drama that was based off of the true story of the Guildford Four and the Maguire Seven. They were a group of people who were wrongly convicted for an act of terrorism in 1974. Their convictions centered around “confessions” that were obtained after days of torture. Years later, the real perpetrators of the bombings came forward, and the police wrote down this evidence with a note on the file: Not to be shown to the defense. As an American, I don’t know the complexity of the situation as well as someone who is actually from Ireland or the UK, but I wanted to try to tell the tale nonetheless - or at least enough of it to provide the context behind the scene. . Movie Scene: https://youtu.be/VI-bKbtlzvo?si=9WFdOFjiraaHaoNF
This is one of the longest posts I’ve ever made. I honestly don’t know how well it’s going to do because of that, but I hope you will enjoy it anyway. Also, please forgive any spelling or grammar errors. I’ve been holding onto this post since 2024, and my brain is FRIED!
Before we can get into the Guildford Four, we have to briefly cover “The Troubles,” which unfortunately I have a very primitive understanding of.
While Ireland and the UK were at war with each other, it was not uncommon for innocent passerby’s to be caught in the crossfire, and severely injured or even killed.
This is my friend Robbie, a Scottish citizen who studied political science and history at university before going back to school to study psychology. I asked him to break the Troubles down for me because I was still struggling to understand it. I do include other sources about The Troubles in my sources cited, of course. Think of this as the "explain like I'm five" summary. Religion was a MASSIVE part in the Troubles overall, but it's not as relevant to the specific incident involving the Guildford Four and Maguire Seven, so I won't be mentioning it again.
It might seem dumb to label an attack as “no warning,” as typically you don’t want to tell the other party that you’re planning to attack them. It would be like the president of one country calling the president of the other country and saying: “Hey, bro, we’re going to attack your military base on Tuesday, at 8:00pm. Look for the firebombing planes that we intend to use, we painted them red.” Like I said in the meme, a no-warning attack is an act of terrorism that is centered around the fact that it was sudden, came out of nowhere, and happened in a place that no one was expecting to people who were uninvolved in the conflict. These types of attack are designed to both kill, and instill paranoia into the target. 9/11, for example, was a “no-warning attack.” Yes, there was evidence of government oversight, but I’m not getting into that. What I mean is that 99% of us did not see the attack coming, the attack involved civilians, and that was precisely the point. The point was to make people afraid of things that are so innocent and innocuous as taking a flight home. . The Guildford Pub Bombings was a no-warning attack. The IRA wanted to send a message that their enemy was much more ruthless than they thought. They wanted England to know that they were literally dead serious about their cause, and they'd keep fighting dirty until they won. Basically, it was a message saying to sleep with one eye open, backed up with evidence of their ruthlesness. . The other element is that TO MY UNDERSTANDING, the IRA normally telephoned the areas warning of the attack to prevent non-combatant deaths. Using explosives to destroy anything is an act of terrorism. Even still, there’s a big difference between destroying a building and eliminating a person. The thing that made the Guildford Pub Bombings so shocking was that it was clearly designed to kill ANY PERSON, not just those participating in the violence. Now for their choice in venue for their attack...
I have read so many articles and reports on the incident, but the exact timeline of events aren’t clearly laid out. Even the official coroner’s report was difficult for me to follow. That might not be the case for everyone - it could just be me having no experience or familiarity with these types of reports. The coroner made it a point to write four paragraphs dedicated to why the bomb definitely went off around 8:50pm, including the sentence: “To put the matter beyond any doubt, I heard oral evidence from Alexander Connor and Rowland Rehill, two soldiers from Bruneval Barracks in Aldershot who were in the (much less busy) Three Pigeons at the time of the blast.” Then they added ANOTHER paragraph on why this was “a matter beyond doubt.” THEN followed up with a very short summary to the effect of: the bomb went off and people died. It must have been terrifying. I FINALLY found one source that wrote it out somewhat clearly, but it’s a website that works similar to Wikipedia. I can’t guarantee 100% accuracy. I did check their sources cited, though. . . Here’s the timeline of events as I came to understand them: On the night October 4th, 1974, the IRA launched their no-warning attack by planting a time-delayed bomb (a bomb that has a timer before going off instead of an immediate blast) in the two different pubs. It is believed that the bombs were planted at 8:30 pm. . . FIRST BLAST At 8:50 pm that evening, the first bomb went off at The Horse and Groom Pub, killing 5 people, and injuring 65 others. The deaths included four off-duty military soldiers: Ann Hamilton (19), Caroline Slater (18), William Forsyth (21), and John Hunter (23)—and one civilian, Paul Craig (22). The blast was especially destructive. Like I said before, the official coroner’s report was excessively detailed and difficult to follow. For this caption, I took out some of the extra words and simplified it to this: “The explosion itself and its immediate effects were violent, intense and devastating. The witnesses spoke of a loud bang and bright flash emanating from the main alcove*, followed by darkness, dust, panic, chaos and confusion. “A number of witnesses in the main alcove also described a feeling of electrocution. This could not have been caused by [the bomb] and was likely the result of live electricity cables falling down with the ceiling.” *an alcove is a small, recessed section of a room with an opening in the shape of an arch. It looks like this: https://bluestoneonline.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/alcove.jpg . . SECOND BLAST Having heard about the bombing at The Horse and Groom, the owners of the other nearby pubs began evacuating the building. This would end up saving so many lives. At 9:25pm, the second bombing went off at the Seven Stars. Because of the evacuations, there were no fatalities. The Survey Line said this about the second blast. Note: This is a British newspaper, so I kept their British spelling. No, Google Docs, it’s not a mistake in spelling. “The Surrey Advertiser reported that Mr O’Brien heard the first blast and left the pub to see what had happened. After seeing the horrific damage, he rushed back to his pub and told everyone to get out. “Brian Pool, a Welsh Guardsman, also worked part time behind the bar at the Seven Stars. He said the pub was busy that evening but emptied after the first explosion when Mr O'Brien told them to leave. They heard there had been an explosion at the Horse and Groom. The two men checked the pub and nearby shop doorways but found nothing. “Minutes later the second bomb went off, injuring Mr O'Brien and his wife, four pub staff and a girl who was walking past. They had searched the pub but could not find anything. The 35-year-old landlord suffered cuts to his head and hands and ended up hospitalised.”
I’ll use a quote from the movie: “Either that man or his superior or his superior's superior ordered that these people be used as scapegoats - by a nation that was baying for blood, in return for the innocent blood spilled on the streets of Guildford! “And by God, you've got your blood, Mr. Dixon. You got the blood of Giuseppe Conlon and the lifeblood of Carole Richardson! You got 15 years of blood and sweat and pain from my client… whose only crime was that he was bloody well Irish! And he was foolish, and he was in the wrong place at the wrong time!”
These people weren’t upstanding citizens that were grabbed on a whim. Obviously the British Government couldn’t get away with that. Gerry Conlon and Paul Hill were petty thieves and drug users. They were living together with a group of squatters when they were arrested. I don’t know why the authorities turned their sights on them specifically. I have scoured the internet, and I can’t find ANYTHING that gives a clear explanation as to why they were arrested. “In The Name of the Father” depicted this as he was doing drugs with Paul in the nearby park when the pubs exploded, however, while this is a biographical film, there are some variations done for artistic reasons. This might be one of them. I know from the coroner’s report that Carole Richardson had been seen in the area at the time of the bombing, but they make no mention of the others. Gerry and Paul were casual friends with Patrick and his seventeen year old girlfriend, Carole Richardson, so that part was guilt by association.
Gerry Conlon identified as “apolitical.” He was at one point a part of an Irish Youth Group called Na Fianna Éireann, but was kicked out due to his frequent petty crimes. Na Fianna Éireann had strong ties to the IRA, but they weren’t actually a part of the IRA. His joining the Na Fianna Éireann doesn’t necessarily mean that he was sympathetic to their cause. This is my understanding of the situation, and I could be off - but I understood Gerry’s involvement in Na Fianna Éireann was his parents’ attempt to reform him because his behavior was getting out of control. Obviously, it didn’t work. Gerry’s petty crimes are especially significant when it came to his suspected involvement with the IRA, because they wouldn’t have accepted him as a member. The IRA was an illegal resistance group. They wanted members that would lie low unless they were in a planned attack. A serial shoplifter and drug addict would’ve brought them unwanted attention that could foil their plans. It is said that Paul Hill was involved with the IRA. I found one news article from an independent journalist that stated this, but couldn’t find anything else to corroborate it. Paul’s letters from prison did seem to suggest that he was at the very least sympathetic to their cause. He was also a suspect in another bombing and a separate murder of a British police officer, but this accusation was tied into the accusation of being a part of the Guildford Bombings. It doesn’t hold much merit. I didn’t find anything that specifically stated that he was in the IRA, so I’m *guessing* that he wasn’t really connected with them. However, there’s a disclaimer that this is a guess. The part that makes this so shocking, cruel, and unthinkable was that there was no connection whatsoever between the accused and the crime. None of these people were involved with the IRA. I don’t even think that they knew anyone in the IRA. It wasn’t even a case where they knew someone involved with the IRA and were subsequently pinned for the crimes of an acquaintance, which while still unjust, would at least make some sort of sense. Literally, and I mean LITERALLY, none of these people were involved in the crime, or the people associated with the crime. The only connections these people had were with each other, and the expulsion from an Irish youth group. The Guildford Four were friends, and The Maguire Seven were related to Gerry Conlon, who was kicked out of said Irish youth group. You know the saying “shoving a square peg into a round hole?” The police basically carved that peg into a circle to force it to fit.
Like with all corrupt governments looking to frame someone, the convictions of the Guildford Four and Maguire Seven were secured through coerced confessions. The coercion was BRUTAL. In light of the bombings, the British Government had quickly passed a law that allowed a suspect of terrorism to be detained for seven days without legal counsel, and law enforcement made very good use of that time. Gerard Conlon, Paul Hill, Patrick Armstrong, and Carole Richardson were tortured with brutal beatings, forced nudity, and sleep deprivation. Gerry finally cracked and “confessed” when the authorities threatened to kill his mother and sister. My understanding is that either Paul or Patrick cracked when one of them was withdrawing from drugs, and interrogators promised to give him methadone if he confessed to the bombings. Paul was also threatened with the harm of his pregnant girlfriend, and was shown the interview room where she was terrified and sobbing. This got him to confess to two different bombings, and the murder of a British officer. Carole was a seventeen year old girl and God knows what they did to her to make her confess. Even the thirteen year old boy from the Maguire Seven was dragged in for questioning and subjected to the same brutality. Regardless of the manner of which these confessions were obtained, they were obtained. The confessions were edited and resubmitted multiple times to make sure that they corroborated with each other.
I don't know if it was an alibi specifically, but other people came forward claiming responsibility for the crime, and the evidence fit their version of events. Oh dear. Now they had a problem: Do they arrest the right people at the cost of being forced to admit their brutal tactics for securing the first arrests? Or do they keep the incident quiet at the cost of knowing that they've put eleven innocent people in jail for sentences ranging from seven years to life without parole? We know which one they picked. They were of course forced to take note of the new confessions, but they didn't investigate it any further, and they slipped a not so subtle note to the prosecution on what to do with their newfound evidence.
For my fellow Americans out there, remember that while the UK has a prime minister, they also have a Queen. Carole’s sentencing was to be kept in prison “at her majesty’s pleasure.” Basically, they would keep her in prison for as long as they wanted, but they could also decide to release her after enough time had passed. It was up to the discretion of the court. She had the opportunity to be released from prison when she was an elderly lady, unlike the others who would probably remain in prison for the rest of their lives. In this extremely unjust situation, there was one stroke of “luck”: Paul, Gerry, and Patrick narrowly avoided the gallows. At the time, treason was a crime that would lead to an execution. The UK didn’t abolished the death penalty altogether until 1998. The judge that presided over their sentencing even said that it was a shame that they weren’t convicted with treason, because they should be hanged for their crimes. Several articles confirm this, but this is what the film specifically said: “It is a pity that you weren’t charged with treason to the crown, a charge which carries a penalty of death by hanging - a sentence that I would have no difficulty impassing in this case.”
Unsurprisingly, the lengthy appeals process was initially met with denial after denial. The courts refused to hear them out. It wasn’t Gareth Pierce took their case that their luck began to change.
It’s hard to turn fifteen years into a two hour movie. Because of that, certain artistic liberties were taken to shorten the timeline of events while holding onto its importance. In 1989, human rights lawyer Gareth Pierce was able to help the Guildford Four get an appeal. She represented Carole, Patrick, and Gerry with this new appeal. Paul Hill had separate legal counsel due to being on trial for the other bombing and the murder of the English Soldier as well. While laying in her bed and reviewing the folder of evidence, the tiny note with “Not to be shown to the defense” fell into Gareth’s lap. She followed the ugly, and falsified paper trail, which exposed the whole conspiracy. The appeals process was long and grueling. The note wasn’t a smoking gun that immediately released these innocent people. It took two trials, and a meticulous review of the evidence. However, that note acted as a footprint that led to more footprints, which led to the eventual discovery of the entire conspiracy.
Here’s what happened after the convictions were overturned. Something that needs to be emphasized: The innocent people being released isn’t a happy ending, it’s a MANDATORY ending. This story does not have a happy ending. It’s not the worst ending as some of these people were able to move on, but it’s not a happy ending either. . . THE UK GOVERNMENT’S RESPONSE TO THE EVENTS: The officers involved in the corruption were tried and acquitted on all charges, unsurprisingly. These same officers resumed their careers, and were later given promotions. Gerry was eventually given a settlement of £50,000. I don’t know if the others were given settlements. In 2005, the British Prime Minister issued a public apology for what the legal system put them through. Mind you that this apology was given sixteen years after these innocent people were released, which was thirty-one years after the horrid miscarriage of justice was committed against them. . . PAUL HILL Paul Hill moved to the United States and married Courtney Kennedy. He became a human rights activist. Tragically, they lost their 22 year old daughter Saoirse Kennedy Hill on October 1st, 2019. She’d died of an accidental overdose after having a serious struggle with depression. . . PADDY ARMSTRONG: For six months after his release, Paddy Armstrong was allowed to live with and work for Alastair Logan, one of the lawyers that represented him. This was to help him acclimate to the massive technological and societal changes that had occurred during his fifteen year incarceration. After that, he moved to Belfast, got married, and has said in a 2024 interview that he is happy and doing well. . . CAROLE RICHARDSON Carole Richardson faded from public view after being released from prison. The only report of her life after her release was she got married, had a daughter, and has since passed away. I don’t know anything more than that. . . GERRY CONLON AND HIS FAMILY Gerry Conlon returned to his family, and they all tried to heal together. He especially struggled, falling into addiction in order to cope with the torture he endured as well as witnessing his equally tortured father die before he could be exonerated. He was eventually able to pick up the pieces of his shattered life, and became a human rights activist. He died from lung cancer on June 21st, 2014, at the age of sixty. . . THE INVESTIGATION INTO THE GUILDFORD BOMBINGS As for the investigation into the bombings, the case was initially just abandoned. In a twist of bitter irony, it was The Guildford Four and Maguire Seven who petitioned to have the investigation re-opened. They wanted to ensure that the guilty ones were both found and brought to justice. In 2017, they were successful in getting the government to resume the investigation. No arrests have been made since the re-opening of the case.
I know that there are a lot of links. I try to keep a balance between proper research, and not bombarding people links upon links, upon links. I always do thorough research on my posts, but I was ESPECIALLY thorough with this one because it involved a lot of moving pieces, contradicting testimony, and political pieces that I didn’t understand. . THE TROUBLES: Britannica Page on The Troubles: https://www.britannica.com/event/The-Troubles-Northern-Ireland-history Britannica Page on The Real Irish Republican Army: https://www.britannica.com/topic/Real-Irish-Republican-Army Robbie: My friend Robbie was a great help in breaking it down a little easier for me A News Report On The Troubles: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5pEPO5JKqTg&list=PLyqRbJF5PWananPNsoZW_XzwjruWrQxS3 ^ This is just a YouTube video of a live news broadcast during The Troubles to show how utterly chaotic and violent it was. . . THE GUILDFORD BOMBINGS The Coroner’s Report: https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/__data/assets/pdf_file/0015/303702/GPB-Findings-and-Conclusion-FOR-WEBSITE.pdf 2021 BBC Article About the Bombings: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-surrey-58753528 Another 2021 BBC Article About the Bombings: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-surrey-57600937 Article on the 50th Anniversary of the bombings: https://www.getsurrey.co.uk/news/surrey-news/guildford-pub-bombings-memorial-service-30082287 2022 Independent News Article About the Bombings: https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/crime/guildford-pub-bomb-ira-coroner-b2128266.html 2022 Update on the Guildford Bombings Investigation: https://www.rte.ie/news/2022/0721/1311500-guildford-pub-bombings/ Another BBC Article on New Evidence in the Investigation: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-surrey-68732819 BBC Article about the Criticism of the Investigation: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-surrey-68739332 Surrey Live Article About the Bombings on its 40th Anniversary: https://www.getsurrey.co.uk/news/surrey-news/guildford-pub-bombings---terrorism-7878429 Grokipedia (the website where I thoroughly checked its sources): https://grokipedia.com/page/Guildford_pub_bombings Wikipedia (helped me summarize things): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guildford_pub_bombings, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guildford_Four_and_Maguire_Seven . . THE GUILDFORD FOUR LA Times Article About the Facts/Falsehoods of The Film: https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-1994-03-07-ca-31174-story.html Nick Davies’ 1989 Article About the Confessions: https://www.nickdavies.net/1986/06/29/the-guildford-four-evidence-of-their-innocence/ BBC Article About Law Enforcement's Corruption: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-surrey-41525570 Interview with Gerry Conlon: http://abcnews.go.com/blogs/politics/2014/06/guildford-fours-gerry-conlon-wrongfully-jailed-for-ira-bombing-dies-at-age-60 Another Interview/Documentary with Gerry Conlon: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hk0P2-u-BA4&t=1643s History Channel: https://www.history.com/this-day-in-history/october-19/guildford-four-are-cleared Miscarriages of Justice Organization (MOJO)’s Overview of the Guildford Four: https://mojoscotland.org/32-years-on-from-the-exoneration-of-the-guildford-four/ Dictionary of Irish Biography’s Article on Gerry Conlon (there were no links for the others): https://www.dib.ie/biography/conlon-gerard-gerry-a10193 Prime Minister's Apology: https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2005/feb/10/northernireland.northernireland An Interview with Paddy Armstrong: https://www.irishtimes.com/culture/stage/2024/12/15/the-guildford-fours-paddy-armstrong-people-thought-i-was-going-to-be-bitter-and-twisted-when-i-came-out-of-prison/ The Guildford Government Website’s Overview on the Guildford Four (in case my wording was too awkward - it’s the official website that’s associated with the UK government): https://www.guildford.org.uk/articles/the-story-of-the-guildford-four/ Irish America’s Article about the 50th Anniversary of the Guildford Four’s Release: https://www.irishamerica.com/2024/10/campaign-for-justice-50-years-on/ A 1977 Article About the Guildford Four (it was an article focusing on their appeal, but I included it because it at least somewhat explained the explained reasons for their specific arrest): https://magill.ie/archive/guildford-four-and-one-law-irish An Article on The Guildford Four and Maguire Seven by One of The Solicitors Who Defended Them: https://www.thejusticegap.com/guildford-four-how-the-innocent-were-framed-and-the-truth-buried/ BBC Article about The Letters Paul Hill Sent from Prison: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-29429010 The Irish Times on Paul Hill’s Letters: https://www.irishtimes.com/life-and-style/abroad/paul-hill-s-letters-burn-with-the-righteousness-of-a-man-jailed-for-crimes-he-did-not-commit-1.3858256 Article about Gerry Conlon’s death in 2014: http://abcnews.go.com/blogs/politics/2014/06/guildford-fours-gerry-conlon-wrongfully-jailed-for-ira-bombing-dies-at-age-60 Paul Hill’s “Eulogy” for Gerry Conlon: https://www.irishcentral.com/roots/history/guildford-four-prison Another Article about Gerry Conlon’s Death: https://web.archive.org/web/20140623233926/http://www.theglobeandmail.com/news/world/gerry-conlon-wrongfully-imprisoned-for-ira-attack-dies-at-60/article19302678/#dashboard/follows/ Saoirse Kennedy Hill's Death: https://www.cnn.com/2019/11/01/politics/saoirse-kennedy-hill-granddaughter-robert-kennedy-death-certificate . . I NEED TO MAKE A DISCLAIMER ABOUT MY USE OF AN INNOCENCE PROJECT AND DEFENSE ATTORNEY’S ARTICLE AS A SOURCES CITED: Typically, a cited source should be an unbiased source that states the facts in an objective manner. I am usually EXTREMELY careful about using innocence projects and defense attorneys as sources cited because both can be unreliable and biased. On the flip side, they could also be telling the truth. There’s a difference between: 1.) Blatant corruption ENSURED that there was no reasonable doubt that someone was guilty 2.) The defense attorney just wasn’t able to prove enough reasonable doubt with the evidence that was given ↑ This can be followed up with: that poor defense attorney’s client insisted on pleading innocent anyway, so they had to come up with something of an argument, even if it sounds insane. I still feel bad for Dahlia Dippolito’s first defense attorney, who was desperate enough to use a reality TV episode defense because she refused to plead guilty despite video evidence of her hiring the hitman that was actually an undercover cop, where she literally said “I’m 5000% sure” when asked if she was sure that she wanted to kill her husband. Testimonies like Dahlia’s are the reason that I make sure that any article featuring an innocence project or a defense attorney is investigated by a third party journalist, or at least a journalist that is highly respected and known for reporting the facts of a case. In this case, the miscarriage of justice was so blatantly obvious that it’s appalling that the people involved in the corruption were never held accountable for it. I don’t see any reason to question a human rights campaign that shows a blatant violation of human rights, especially when they are supported by respectable sources.
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd
